description: Whether other outdoor dining locations appear close by.
How active the surrounding food and nightlife environment is.
role: Acts as a proxy for local restaurant activity.

description: Whether 311 complaints appear near outdoor dining locations.
role: Shows where outdoor dining overlapped with reported street issues.

description: Local street conditions that may affect where outdoor dining can fit.
Roadway cafes operate under seasonal rules in the permanent program.
role: Explains why the permanent program differs from the emergency program.

description: Outdoor dining must leave required pedestrian space.
role: Limits where sidewalk dining can physically fit.

description: New rules shape what can be built and where it can be placed.
role: Explains why some locations may not continue under the new program.

description: Restaurants must apply under the permanent Dining Out NYC program.
Outdoor dining structures may need to be installed, removed, or redesigned.
Seasonal roadway setups create storage needs during off months.
role: Adds a practical challenge for restaurants.

description: Fees, materials, labor, and maintenance affect participation.
role: Explains why continuation may not be realistic for every restaurant.

description: Outdoor spaces require cleaning, repairs, and upkeep.
role: Connects the program to sanitation and street management.

description: Restaurants decide whether to apply, rebuild, maintain, or stop outdoor dining.
role: Acknowledges decisions that are not visible in public datasets.

description: Local support or opposition may shape how outdoor dining is received.
role: Connects public response to local conditions.

description: Some streets can absorb outdoor dining more easily than others.
role: Connects the model back to street level visibility.
